item.title: Peripheral Immune Challenge Drives Enteric α-Synuclein and Tau Pathology in LRRK2 G2019S Mice.

item.title: Risks of Undertreatment and Overtreatment in Pediatric Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Navigating the Balance.

item.title: Safety and efficacy of advanced combination therapies for treating inflammatory bowel disease in adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

item.title: Between-visit care represents a substantial and underrecognized component of health care utilization in pediatric inflammatory bowel disease.

item.title: Histological activity predicts relapse in pediatric ulcerative colitis despite mucosal healing: a multicenter study from the pediatric IBD Porto group of ESPGHAN.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Multicenter study - The STRIDE-II recommends mucosal healing as a treatment goal for patients with ulcerative colitis (UC), yet histological remission is increasingly recognized as a meaningful therapeutic target. Thi...

item.title: Intestinal spirochetosis in the modern era: a decade-long clinicopathologic study.
item.description: American journal of clinical pathology - Intestinal spirochetosis (IS) is infrequently identified in about 1% of human colonic biopsy specimens. On routine sections, it is diagnosed by identifying a thick band of basophilia on the apical ...

item.title: Is Routine Monitoring of TNF-α Inhibitor Levels and Antibodies in Pediatric IBD Justified in the Era of Personalized Medicine.

item.title: Pediatric health: mechanistic insights into inflammatory diseases and emerging therapeutic pathway-targeted approaches.
